question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 30.00% on 2026-03-28 for the Metaculus question "Will the United States experience negative GDP growth during Q1, Q2, or Q3 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on US negative GDP growth (Q1-Q3 2026) will exceed 30% on March 28. The CP is currently exactly at 30.00% with strong upward momentum (+6pp in 2 weeks), driven by the US-Iran conflict, oil shock (Strait of Hormuz closed since March 3, Brent at ~$100/bbl), and weak February jobs data (-92,000 payrolls). External recession estimates (Goldman 25%, Moody's ~50%, Polymarket ~34%) and the fact the underlying question covers 3 quarters (broader than recession) suggest the CP at 30% is somewhat underpriced relative to fundamentals (~35-45% fair value). With zero buffer (CP exactly at threshold), ongoing conflict catalysts, and upcoming data releases including the Fed meeting, there's meaningful upward pressure. A weighted Monte Carlo simulation across drift scenarios yielded ~67%. Main downside risk is a rapid conflict resolution or full pricing-in of current information.

factors.description: Strong upward CP momentum: +6pp in 2 weeks (24% to 30%), with the most recent week showing +4pp. This is the strongest directional signal for continued movement above threshold.

Ongoing Iran conflict and oil shock (Strait of Hormuz closed since March 3, oil at $98-103/bbl) provides persistent negative economic catalyst that should continue pushing recession fears higher over the next 11 days.

Fundamentals suggest CP is underpriced: external recession estimates at 25-50%, and the Metaculus Q covers 3 quarters (broader than recession), suggesting fair value ~35-45%. This supports continued upward drift.
Weak February jobs data (-92,000 payrolls, 4.4% unemployment) and volatile GDPNow (dropped from 3.2% to 2.1% before recovering to 2.7%) signal economic fragility that reinforces recession concerns.
Zero buffer: CP is exactly at 30.00%. ANY net upward movement crosses the threshold. This asymmetry favors YES given the existing upward momentum.
Fed meeting March 17-18 and upcoming economic data releases provide additional catalysts that could push CP higher.
Potential for conflict resolution or ceasefire in next 11 days could reverse momentum. If Iran conflict resolves, oil drops and recession fears ease significantly.
Information may already be priced in: CP rose sharply in recent weeks as the Iran conflict developed. Further upward movement requires NEW negative information beyond what is already known.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.65 (65% probability CP will be above 30% on March 28)**

Key reasoning:
- **CP at exact threshold** (30.00%) with **strong upward momentum** (+6pp in 2 weeks)
- **Ongoing Iran conflict** and oil shock provide persistent negative economic catalysts
- **Fundamentals favor higher CP**: external recession odds at 25-50%, and the question covers 3 quarters (more permissive than "recession")
- **Zero buffer** means any small upward movement resolves YES
- **Downside risk**: conflict resolution or full information pricing could stabilize/reverse CP
- **Weighted Monte Carlo simulation** across drift scenarios: ~67%, which I round slightly down to 65% accounting for potential information saturation

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41089) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-28 13:48:16 is higher than 30.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 30.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-28 13:48:16, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
